captured.evidence: Lidocaine has a high extraction ratio meaning its clearance depends on hepatic blood flow. In cardiogenic shock, blood flow to the liver is reduced so the drug accumulates regardless of enzyme function.
For renal clearance, drugs are filtered at the glomerulus, some are actively secreted, and some are reabsorbed.
The creatinine is misleading because she has low muscle mass producing less creatinine. A normal level does not mean normal kidney function in the elderly. You need Cockcroft-Gault equations.

missed.description: Ion trapping mechanisms and clinical applications

summary: Solid understanding of hepatic extraction ratios and age-related creatinine changes with appropriate clinical application. Missing discussion of ion trapping and phase II metabolism concepts.
voss_quip: Good basics, but you're only scratching the surface of pharmacokinetics.
